name: Burn
description: Deals damage twice per second. Each tick the value decreases by 1 (decaying). A Burn of 10 deals 10+9+8...+1 = 55 total damage.
interactions: Shield reduces Burn damage by 50%
Heal removes 1 Burn per heal trigger
Does NOT stack additively — multiple Burns add their totals separately
Fiery enchantment applies Burn equal to 10% of the item's damage
edgeCase: Burn and Poison apply simultaneously — a board with both can bypass both Shield and healing defenses.

name: Poison
description: Deals a fixed amount of damage every second. Does NOT decay — each tick deals the full Poison value.
interactions: Completely bypasses Shield — Poison is unblockable
Countered ONLY by Regeneration or Healing
Heal removes 1 Poison per heal trigger
Toxic enchantment applies Poison equal to 10% of the item's damage
edgeCase: Unlike Burn, Poison bypasses Shield entirely. A 50 Poison player takes 50 damage per second regardless of how much shield they have.

name: Freeze
description: Stops an item's cooldown from advancing for the duration. The frozen item cannot activate while frozen.
interactions: Flying items have Freeze duration halved
Radiant enchantment makes an item immune to Freeze
Icy enchantment applies Freeze for 1 second
Dooley's chain-trigger items are completely shut down while frozen
Freezing an enemy's high-damage item can decide fights
edgeCase: Freezing Dooley's Core breaks his entire trigger chain. This is the single best counter to Dooley builds.

name: Haste
description: The targeted item charges at double the normal rate for the duration. Duration-based.
interactions: Turbo enchantment applies Haste to 2 items for 2 seconds
Stellear Swallowtail charges when any item gains Haste
Pill Buggy (Stelle) gains Shield when items gain Haste
Haste enables fast-cycling boards — key to exponential damage
Tortuga (Vanessa) gives 1s Haste to ALL items on board
edgeCase: Haste and Slow cannot coexist — Haste overrides Slow and vice versa based on which applied last.

name: Slow
description: The targeted item charges at half the normal rate for the duration.
interactions: Flying items have Slow duration halved
Heavy enchantment applies Slow for 1 second to 2 items
Chains (Karnok) applies Slow
Can disable key items that require fast cycling
Boa Constrictor (Karnok) scales damage based on Slow applied
edgeCase: Slowing an opponent's lowest-cooldown item (their 'engine' item) can delay their entire combo chain significantly.

name: Shield
description: Absorbs incoming damage. Reduces Burn damage by 50%. Does NOT block Poison.
interactions: Shielded enchantment applies Shield on item activation
Durian (Jules) converts current Shield amount into Damage
Pyg's Gym and ATM generate Shield
Armored Core (Dooley) grants Shield on left-item activation
Shield completely blocks physical damage and reduces Burn, but is useless vs Poison
counters: Durian converts shield into damage against you if opponent has Durian
edgeCase: Durian (Jules) turns YOUR high Shield value into damage against you. Fighting a Durian Jules board — don't let your shield stack too high.

name: Regen
description: Restores a fixed HP amount per second. Counters Poison tick-for-tick.
interactions: Counters Poison — each Regen tick reduces effective Poison damage
Bread Knife (Jules) converts Regen stacks into Damage
Philosopher's Stone (Mak) gains permanent +Regen each time a Reagent is transformed
Restorative enchantment applies Heal (not Regen) on activation
Staff of the Moose applies Regen + scales damage off health thresholds
counters: Burn (removes 1 Burn but doesn't reduce damage already applied)
Very high Poison can outpace Regen
edgeCase: Regen and Heal are different: Regen is per-second flat restoration; Heal restores to maximum HP and removes status effects.

name: Heal
description: Restores HP to maximum. Removes 1 Poison AND 1 Burn per trigger.
interactions: Removes 1 Poison per trigger (unlike Regen which counteracts per second)
Removes 1 Burn per trigger
Triggers skill effects with 'when you Heal' conditions
Lifesteal does NOT count as Healing for skill trigger purposes — critical edge case
Restorative enchantment applies Heal on item activation
counters: Multiple simultaneous Poison/Burn stacks overwhelm single heal triggers
edgeCase: IMPORTANT: Lifesteal does NOT count as Healing. If your skill says 'when you Heal, do X' — Lifesteal will NOT proc it. This changes many Mak skill combo calculations.

name: Charge
description: Instantly advances an item's cooldown by X seconds, making it available to fire faster.
interactions: The Core (Dooley) charges all items to the right by 1 second
Haste speeds up charging rate; Charge skips ahead instantly
Crucial for activating high-value large items earlier
Turbo enchantment applies Haste (not Charge) — different mechanic
counters: Freeze stops charged items from advancing further
edgeCase: Charge bypasses the cooldown timer entirely for that amount — a 2-second Charge on a 4-second CD item makes it fire at the 2-second mark instead.

name: Multicast
description: The item activates multiple times per use. Each activation from Multicast counts as a SEPARATE item use.
interactions: Each Multicast counts as a separate item use — affects ALL 'when you use this item' triggers
Robotic Factory (Dooley) gives all Friend items Multicast
Shiny enchantment adds +1 Multicast
Multicast on Core items multiplies chain-trigger effects
Throwing Knives and Yoyo appear to have no internal cooldown — Multicast freely
edgeCase: Multicast and trigger-on-use items create exponential scaling. A 3× Multicast item with 'when used, charge right neighbor 1s' charges that neighbor 3× per activation.

name: Lifesteal
description: Regains HP proportional to damage dealt.
interactions: Does NOT count as Healing for skill trigger purposes
Does NOT remove Poison or Burn like Heal does
Mak has more Lifesteal access than any other hero
Rapid Injection System (Mak) features Lifesteal + self-poison
counters: Deals no HP recovery against Poison damage
edgeCase: The most misunderstood keyword. 'Lifesteal does NOT count as Healing' — this is critical for skill builds. Any skill that procs on Heal will not fire from Lifesteal.

name: Flying
description: Item has the Flying property. Slow and Freeze durations applied to Flying items are halved.
interactions: Slow duration halved on flying items
Freeze duration halved on flying items
Skyliner gains +1 Multicast per flying item on board
Hangar grants +10 Damage and +10 Shield to all items (flying or not)
Two trigger states: 'Start Flying' and 'Stop Flying' create a cycling loop for Stelle
counters: Freeze still applies at half duration
edgeCase: Start Flying ≠ Stop Flying. Building only start-triggers or only stop-triggers halves your potential. True cycling requires both.

name: Ammo
description: A limited-use counter on weapons. When depleted, the item stops charging. Fully refills after each fight.
interactions: Ammo depletes with each activation, then the item goes dormant
Reload items (and reload skills) replenish ammo mid-fight
Fully refills between every fight automatically
Arbalest, Hunting Rifle, and many Vanessa weapons use Ammo
Karnok's Hunting Rifle has 2 Ammo charges post-Season 14 buff
counters: Running out of ammo mid-fight is a hard counter to ammo-dependent builds
edgeCase: In very long fights (vs. high-HP boards), ammo depletion can effectively disable your best weapon. Carry reload items or avoid full ammo-dependency on slow boards.

name: Crit Chance
description: Percentage chance to double ALL effects of an item activation simultaneously — damage, poison, heal, regen, shield, AND burn.
interactions: Crit doubles ALL effects at once, not just damage
Deadly enchantment adds +50% Crit Chance
Critical Core (Dooley) increases crit chance for itself and items to the right
Magic Carpet (Mak) scales damage on crits
Birthday Cake (Jules) was buffed to 30% Crit Chance in Season 14
edgeCase: A crit on a Multicast item doubles EACH multicast activation separately — this compounds exponentially with high-multicast builds.
